structured.title: Family Decides On Pizza Order
structured.overview: A family discusses what to order for a takeaway pizza meal. They decide on three large pizzas, including options like ham and pineapple, farmhouse, and a meaty pizza, debating whether this will provide enough slices and some leftovers. They weigh promotional offers (10 pounds off £30 vs 40% off), conclude that sides like chips and garlic bread are poor value, and opt not to add many sides beyond possibly chicken, which the boys like. They confirm the final cost after discount, briefly mention probiotics with melon and their expiration dates, a small charity donation prompt at checkout, which payment card to use (asking Rachel), and note that Bradley slept better the previous night and that a UK good news source has many positive stories.

apps_results.content:
## Pizza choices
- Decided on three large pizzas total  
- Chosen types: mighty meaty, ham & pineapple, farmhouse  
- Plain cheese pizza option briefly considered

## Portions and quantity
- One large serves about 3–4 people  
- Debated three vs four pizzas; settled on three  
- Expect some leftovers with three larges

## Deals and pricing
- Chose 40% off offer, no minimum spend  
- Total before discount: £39.98  
- Final cost still cheaper than Wetherspoons

## Sides discussion
- Sides mostly seen as poor value  
- Chips/fries considered awful; chicken strips liked but pricey  
- Decided to skip sides; dips come with pizzas

## Payment and small donation
- Chose specific card (“the decode”) for payment  
- Considered 2p charity donation; likely agreed  
- Checked probiotic melon dates: good for 6–12 months

## General updates
- Bradley slept better last night  
- Others also slept better  
- Mention of seeing “good news” UK stories

## Next steps
- Place order for three large pizzas  
- Pay with chosen card, including small donation  
- Wait for delivery and enjoy meal
